2603.17420.title: From Digital Twins to World Models:Opportunities, Challenges, and Applications for Mobile Edge General Intelligence
2603.17420.paper_summary: The rapid evolution toward 6G and beyond communication systems is accelerating the convergence of digital twins and world models at the network edge. Traditional digital twins provide high-fidelity representations of physical systems and support monitoring, analysis, and offline optimization. However, in highly dynamic edge environments, they face limitations in autonomy, adaptability, and scalability. This paper presents a systematic survey of the transition from digital twins to world models and discusses its role in enabling edge general intelligence (EGI). First, the paper clarifies the conceptual differences between digital twins and world models and highlights the shift from physics-based, centralized, and system-centric replicas to data-driven, decentralized, and agent-centric internal models. This discussion helps readers gain a clear understanding of how this transition enables more adaptive, autonomous, and resource-efficient intelligence at the network edge. The paper reviews the design principles, architectures, and key components of world models, including perception, latent state representation, dynamics learning, imagination-based planning, and memory. In addition, it examines the integration of world models and digital twins in wireless EGI systems and surveys emerging applications in integrated sensing and communications, semantic communication, air-ground networks, and low-altitude wireless networks. Finally, this survey provides a systematic roadmap and practical insights for designing world-model-driven edge intelligence systems in wireless and edge computing environments. It also outlines key research challenges and future directions toward scalable, reliable, and interoperable world models for edge-native agentic AI.
2603.17420.paper_summary_zh: 快速演進的6G及更高階通訊系統正在加速數位雙胞胎與世界模型在網路邊緣的融合。傳統的數位雙胞胎提供物理系統的高保真表示，並支持監控、分析和離線優化。然而，在高度動態的邊緣環境中，它們在自主性、適應性和可擴展性方面面臨限制。本文系統性地調查了從數位雙胞胎到世界模型的過渡，並討論了其在促進邊緣通用智能（EGI）中的角色。首先，本文澄清了數位雙胞胎與世界模型之間的概念差異，並強調了從基於物理的、集中式的和系統中心的複製品轉變為基於數據的、去中心化的和以代理為中心的內部模型的過程。這一討論幫助讀者清晰理解這一過渡如何使網路邊緣的智能變得更具適應性、自主性和資源效率。本文回顧了世界模型的設計原則、架構和關鍵組件，包括感知、潛在狀態表示、動態學習、基於想像的規劃和記憶。此外，還檢視了世界模型與數位雙胞胎在無線EGI系統中的整合，並調查了在集成感知與通訊、語意通訊、空地網路和低空無線網路中的新興應用。最後，這項調查提供了一個系統的路線圖和實用的見解，以設計在無線和邊緣計算環境中以世界模型為驅動的邊緣智能系統。它還概述了邊緣本地代理AI的可擴展、可靠和互操作的世界模型的關鍵研究挑戰和未來方向。
2603.17420.author: Jie Zheng et.al.
2603.17420.authors: Jie Zheng, Dusit Niyato, Changyuan Zhao, Jiawen Kang, Jiacheng Wang
